Title: Make Directory
Description: Create a directory and all intermediate parents

Title: Remove Directory
Description: Recursively delete a directory and all its contents

Title: List Files (detailed)
Description: List all files with human-readable sizes

Title: List Files (tree)
Description: Show directory contents as a tree up to given depth

Title: Copy File
Description: Copy a file or directory to a new location

Title: Copy Recursive
Description: Recursively copy a directory and all its contents

Title: Move / Rename
Description: Move or rename a file or directory

Title: Delete File
Description: Remove a single file permanently

Title: Create Empty File
Description: Create an empty file or update its modification time

Title: Find Files by Name
Description: Search the entire filesystem for files matching a pattern

Title: Find Files in Directory
Description: Search a specific directory for matching files

Title: Find Files Modified Recently
Description: Find files modified within the last N days

Title: Find Large Files
Description: Find files larger than N megabytes

Title: Show File Contents
Description: Print the full contents of a file to stdout

Title: Show File Head
Description: Print the first N lines of a file

Title: Show File Tail
Description: Print the last N lines of a file

Title: Tail Log File
Description: Stream new lines as they are appended to a log file

Title: Page Through File
Description: Interactively scroll through file contents

Title: Word / Line Count
Description: Count the number of lines in a file

Title: File Type Info
Description: Determine and print the type of a file

Title: File / Inode Details
Description: Show detailed metadata: size, permissions, timestamps, inode

Title: Disk Usage of Directory
Description: Show total disk usage of a directory in human-readable form

Title: Disk Usage Sorted
Description: Show top-20 largest subdirectories sorted by size

Title: Set File Permissions
Description: Change read/write/execute permissions on a file

Title: Set Permissions Recursive
Description: Recursively change permissions on a directory tree

Title: Change Owner
Description: Change the owning user and group of a file

Title: Change Owner Recursive
Description: Recursively change ownership of a directory tree

Title: Create Symlink
Description: Create a symbolic link pointing to a target path

Title: Archive with tar
Description: Compress a folder into a gzipped tarball archive

Title: Extract tar.gz
Description: Extract a gzipped tarball to the current directory

Title: Extract tar.gz to Path
Description: Extract a gzipped tarball to a specific destination directory

Title: Zip Files
Description: Create a zip archive from a folder

Title: Unzip Archive
Description: Extract a zip archive to a destination directory

Title: Rsync Local
Description: Sync files locally with progress output

Title: Rsync to Remote
Description: Sync files to a remote host over SSH with compression

Title: Diff Two Files
Description: Show unified diff between two files

Title: Search Text in File
Description: Find lines matching a pattern with line numbers

Title: Search Recursively
Description: Recursively search all files under a path for a pattern

Title: Case-Insensitive Search
Description: Search for a pattern ignoring upper/lower case

Title: Search with Context
Description: Show matching lines plus 3 lines of surrounding context

Title: Sed Replace In-Place
Description: Replace all occurrences of a string inside a file

Title: Awk Print Column
Description: Print a specific column from a whitespace-delimited file

Title: Sort File
Description: Sort lines of a file alphabetically

Title: Sort Unique
Description: Sort lines and remove duplicates

Title: Count Occurrences
Description: Count and rank the most frequent lines in a file

Title: Cut Fields
Description: Extract specific fields from each line using a delimiter

Title: Translate Characters
Description: Replace or delete characters read from a file

Title: Ping Host
Description: Send 4 ICMP echo requests to test connectivity

Title: Show IP Addresses
Description: Display IP addresses for all network interfaces

Title: Show Routing Table
Description: Print the kernel routing table

Title: My IP Address
Description: Print the current machine IP address (system variable)

Title: Test Port
Description: Check if a specific TCP port is open on a remote host

Title: Download File (wget)
Description: Download a file from a URL using wget

Title: Download File (curl)
Description: Download a file from a URL using curl

Title: HTTP Headers (curl)
Description: Fetch and display only the HTTP response headers

Title: curl with JSON
Description: POST a JSON payload and print the response

Title: Show Open Ports
Description: List all listening TCP ports with associated process names

Title: Trace Route
Description: Print network hops between this machine and a destination

Title: DNS Lookup
Description: Query DNS records for a domain name

Title: Reverse DNS Lookup
Description: Resolve an IP address back to its hostname

Title: nslookup
Description: Query a DNS server for a hostname or IP address

Title: SSH to Host
Description: Open an SSH session using the current username

Title: SSH with Key
Description: Connect via SSH using a specific identity file

Title: SSH Port Forward
Description: Create an SSH local port-forwarding tunnel

Title: SCP Copy to Remote
Description: Securely copy a local file to a remote server

Title: SCP Copy from Remote
Description: Securely copy a file from a remote server

Title: Generate SSH Key
Description: Generate a new Ed25519 SSH key pair

Title: Copy SSH Key to Server
Description: Install a public SSH key on a remote server

Title: Firewall Status
Description: Show current UFW firewall rules and their status

Title: UFW Allow Port
Description: Open a TCP port in the UFW firewall

Title: UFW Deny Port
Description: Block a TCP port in the UFW firewall

Title: iptables List Rules
Description: List all iptables rules with packet/byte counters

Title: Nmap Port Scan
Description: Scan specific ports on a host (requires nmap)

Title: Nmap Service Scan
Description: Detect services and versions running on open ports

Title: System Update (apt)
Command: sudo apt update && sudo apt upgrade -y
Description: Refresh package list and upgrade all installed packages

Title: Reboot
Description: Restart the system immediately

Title: Shutdown
Description: Power off the machine without delay

Title: Memory Usage
Description: Display RAM and swap usage in human-readable form

Title: CPU & Load Snapshot
Description: Print a one-shot snapshot of CPU usage and load

Title: Disk Space
Description: Show free and used disk space for all mounted filesystems

Title: System Info
Description: Print kernel name, version, and architecture

Title: Hostname
Description: Show or set the system hostname and OS details

Title: Set Hostname
Description: Permanently change the machine hostname

Title: Current Date/Time
Description: Print the current system date and time

Title: Uptime
Description: Show how long the system has been running and load average

Title: CPU Info
Description: Display CPU architecture information

Title: Hardware Info
Description: Print a short summary of hardware components

Title: PCI Devices
Description: List all PCI devices in the system

Title: USB Devices
Description: List all USB devices currently attached

Title: Block Devices
Description: List block devices with filesystem type and mount point

Title: Mount Point Info
Description: Show all currently mounted filesystems in tabular form

Title: Mount Disk
Description: Mount a block device at a given mount point

Title: Unmount Disk
Description: Unmount a filesystem from its mount point

Title: Kernel Ring Buffer
Description: Show the last 50 kernel log messages

Title: Running Services
Description: List all currently active and running systemd services

Title: Service Status
Description: Show current status and recent log of a systemd service

Title: Start Service
Description: Start a systemd service immediately

Title: Stop Service
Description: Stop a running systemd service

Title: Restart Service
Description: Restart a named systemd service immediately

Title: Reload Service
Description: Ask a service to reload its configuration without restarting

Title: Enable Service on Boot
Description: Mark a service to start automatically on boot

Title: Disable Service
Description: Prevent a service from starting automatically on boot

Title: Journal Logs
Description: Show recent systemd journal entries with context lines

Title: Journal for Service
Description: Follow live log output for a specific systemd service

Title: Journal Since Boot
Description: Show all journal entries since the last boot

Title: Failed Services
Description: List all systemd units that have entered a failed state

Title: Time & Timezone
Description: Show system date, time, timezone and NTP sync status

Title: Set Timezone
Description: Change the system timezone (e.g. Europe/Amsterdam)

Title: Who Is Logged In
Description: Show a list of currently logged-in users

Title: Last Logins
Description: Display the 20 most recent login records

Title: Environment Variables
Description: Print all environment variables sorted alphabetically

Title: Watch Command
Description: Run a command repeatedly every N seconds

Title: IO Statistics
Description: Show extended disk I/O statistics every second, 5 times

Title: Virtual Memory Stats
Description: Print virtual memory, I/O and CPU stats 5 times at 1s intervals

Title: Open Files by Process
Description: List all files and sockets opened by a process ID

Title: Open Files on Port
Description: Show which process is listening on a given port

Title: Install Package (apt)
Description: Install one or more packages via apt non-interactively

Title: Remove Package
Description: Remove a package and its configuration files

Title: Search Package
Description: Search available apt packages by keyword

Title: Package Info
Description: Display detailed information about an apt package

Title: List Installed Packages
Description: List installed packages matching a search pattern

Title: Which Files a Package Owns
Description: List all files installed by a package

Title: Which Package Owns File
Description: Find which package provides a specific file

Title: Autoremove Unused
Description: Remove packages that are no longer needed as dependencies

Title: Install Package (yum)
Description: Install a package via yum (RHEL/CentOS/Fedora)

Title: Install Package (dnf)
Description: Install a package via dnf (Fedora/RHEL 8+)

Title: List All Processes
Description: Show all running processes with CPU and memory usage

Title: Process Tree
Description: Display running processes as a tree with PIDs

Title: Kill Process by Name
Description: Send SIGTERM to all processes matching the given name

Title: Kill by PID
Description: Forcefully terminate a process by its process ID

Title: Find Process by Name
Description: Find PIDs and command lines matching a process name

Title: Interactive Process Viewer
Description: Launch interactive process viewer (requires htop installed)

Title: Run in Background
Description: Run a command immune to hangup signals, in background

Title: Nice Priority
Description: Start a command with adjusted CPU scheduling priority (-20 to 19)

Title: Renice Process
Description: Change the scheduling priority of a running process

Title: Strace Process
Description: Trace system calls made by a running process

Title: Add User
Description: Create a new user account interactively

Title: Delete User
Description: Remove a user account and their home directory

Title: Change Password
Description: Set or change a user's password

Title: Add User to Group
Description: Add a user to a supplementary group (e.g. sudo, docker)

Title: List Groups
Description: Show all groups a user belongs to

Title: Current User Info
Description: Print UID, GID and group memberships for the current user

Title: Add Group
Description: Create a new user group

Title: Switch User
Description: Switch to another user account with a login shell

Title: List sudo Privileges
Description: List commands the current user may run with sudo

Title: Git Status
Description: Show the working tree status and staged changes

Title: Git Log
Description: Show last 20 commits as a compact graph across all branches

Title: Git Diff
Description: Show unstaged changes in the working tree

Title: Git Diff Staged
Description: Show changes that are staged for the next commit

Title: Git Pull
Description: Fetch and merge changes from the upstream branch

Title: Git Push
Description: Push local commits to the remote repository

Title: Git Add All
Description: Stage all changes (new, modified and deleted files)

Title: Git Commit
Description: Create a commit with a message

Title: Git Clone
Description: Clone a remote repository into a new local directory

Title: Git Checkout Branch
Description: Switch to an existing branch

Title: Git New Branch
Description: Create and switch to a new branch

Title: Git Stash
Description: Save the current working tree changes to a stash

Title: Git Stash Pop
Description: Apply the most recent stash and remove it from the stash list

Title: Git Reset Hard
Description: Discard all local changes and restore HEAD state

Title: Git Remote List
Description: List configured remote repositories with their URLs

Title: SSL Certificate Info
Description: Check the expiry dates of an HTTPS certificate

Title: Generate Self-Signed Cert
Description: Create a self-signed TLS certificate valid for 365 days

Title: Decode JWT Token
Description: Pretty-print the payload section of a JWT token

Title: Hash File (SHA256)
Description: Compute the SHA-256 checksum of a file

Title: Certbot Renew
Description: Test Let's Encrypt certificate renewal without making changes

Title: MySQL Connect
Description: Connect to a MySQL/MariaDB database as a user

Title: MySQL Dump
Description: Export a MySQL database to a SQL file

Title: MySQL Import
Description: Import a SQL file into a MySQL database

Title: PostgreSQL Connect
Description: Connect to a PostgreSQL database

Title: PostgreSQL Dump
Description: Export a PostgreSQL database to a SQL file

Title: SQLite Open
Description: Open or create an SQLite database file

Title: Crontab Edit
Description: Open the current user's crontab for editing

Title: Crontab List
Description: List all cron jobs for the current user

Title: System Cron Jobs
Description: Show system-wide crontab and cron.d entries

Title: Nginx Reload
Command: sudo nginx -t && sudo systemctl reload nginx
Description: Test nginx configuration then reload it if valid

Title: Apache Reload
Command: sudo apachectl configtest && sudo systemctl reload apache2
Description: Test Apache configuration then reload it if valid

Title: tmux New Session
Description: Create a new named tmux session

Title: tmux List Sessions
Description: Show all active tmux sessions

Title: tmux Attach
Description: Reconnect to an existing tmux session by name

Title: Screen New Session
Description: Start a new named GNU screen session

Title: Screen List
Description: List all active GNU screen sessions

Title: Screen Attach
Description: Reconnect to a detached screen session
